summary: pre-1 check failed: task 162 OptionSome/OptionNone variants absent from codebase. Escalated as esc-163-25. No implementation steps taken.

Added OptionSome/OptionNone variants to CompiledExprKind in reify-types, plus walk/collect/remap arms and constructors. Added stub eval_expr arms and option_eval_tests.rs with step-1 tests. Blocked: reify-compiler/src/lib.rs:collect_body_refs_inner() also matches on CompiledExprKind exhaustively and needs OptionSome/OptionNone arms — outside task scope. Escalated.

All 6 steps complete. Fixed reify-compiler collect_body_refs_inner exhaustive match (OptionSome/OptionNone arms). Implemented OptionNone => Value::Option(None) and OptionSome(inner) => Value::Option(Some(Box::new(eval(inner)))) in eval_expr. 12 tests in option_eval_tests.rs all pass. Full reify-expr suite clean.

All 6 prerequisites and 14 steps complete. Grammar changes: added qualified_access (prec 2) and instance_qualified_access (prec 8) rules to grammar.js, regenerated parser. AST: added QualifiedAccess and InstanceQualifiedAccess ExprKind variants. Lowering: lower_qualified_access and lower_instance_qualified_access methods. 7 tests all pass. Full reify-syntax suite clean. Side fix: added diagnostic stub arms for new variants in reify-compiler exhaustive match (outside declared scope, escalated as esc-191-1).

Create crates/reify-compiler/tests/trait_merge_tests.rs with helpers (compile_module, compile_first_template) and 2 tests: (a) let_defaults_same_name_same_expr_merge — two traits each with `let area : Real = width * height`, structure implements both, expect 0 errors and exactly 1 'area' value cell. (b) param_requirements_same_name_same_type_merge — two traits each requiring `param x : Length`, structure provides `param x : Length = 5mm`, expect 0 errors (existing behavior, baseline test).; Add failing test: let_defaults_same_name_different_type_error — trait A has `let x : Length = 5mm`, trait B has `let x : Mass = 1kg`, structure implements both. Expect error diagnostic containing 'conflicting'. This will FAIL because DefaultKind::Let uses Type::Real sentinel, making both types appear identical.; Change DefaultKind::Let(LetDecl) to DefaultKind::Let { cell_type: Type, let_decl: LetDecl }. Update compile_trait() to pass the resolved type into the new struct variant. Update all pattern match sites in check_trait_conformance(): (1) pre-registration loop (line ~4178) — use cell_type instead of Type::Real; (2) injection loop (line ~4211) — destructure Let { cell_type, let_decl } and use cell_type for the injected ValueCellDecl. Update collect_all_requirements() — extract cell_type from DefaultKind::Let { cell_type, .. } instead of using Type::Real sentinel. Step-2 test should now pass.; Add failing test: let_defaults_same_name_same_type_different_expr_error — trait A has `let x : Real = width + 1`, trait B has `let x : Real = width * 2`, structure implements both with `param width : Real = 5`. Expect error diagnostic mentioning 'conflicting' and 'expression'. This will FAIL because collect_all_requirements only checks type, not expression content.; Add expression comparison for let defaults in collect_all_requirements. Change seen_defaults from HashMap<String, Type> to HashMap<String, (Type, Option<ContentHash>)>. For DefaultKind::Let, store (cell_type, Some(let_decl.content_hash)). For Param/Constraint, store (type, None). On collision: if both have Some(hash) and hashes differ while types match → emit 'conflicting let expressions' error. Update the signature of seen_defaults and all its access sites. Step-4 test should now pass.; Add failing test: requirement_satisfied_by_cross_trait_default — trait A requires `param x : Length` (no default), trait B provides `param x : Length = 10mm` (default). Structure implements A + B with empty body. Expect 0 errors (the default from B satisfies A's requirement). This will FAIL because check_trait_conformance only checks requirements against structure_members, not against available defaults.; In check_trait_conformance, before the requirement-checking loop (line ~4114), build a HashMap<String, Type> of available default names from all_defaults (excluding constraints). In the None arm of the requirement check (line ~4132), before emitting 'missing required member', check if the requirement name exists in the defaults map with a matching type. If so, skip the error (the default will satisfy the requirement during injection). If the default exists but with a different type, emit a type mismatch error. Step-6 test should now pass.; Add test: let_conflict_resolved_by_structure_override — trait A has `let x : Real = a + 1`, trait B has `let x : Real = a * 2`, structure implements both and provides its own `let x = a + a`. Expect 0 errors (structure override resolves the conflict). Also add: constraints_compose_conjunctively_across_traits — trait A has `constraint x > 0mm`, trait B has `constraint x < 100mm`, structure provides param x, expect both constraints injected (>= 2 constraints).; If step-8 tests fail: For let conflict resolution by override, update collect_all_requirements to check structure_members before reporting let expression conflict (similar to how default type conflicts already check structure_members at line ~4354). For constraint composition, verify existing behavior is correct (should already work via unnamed defaults always-push logic). Fix any remaining issues.
